name: wisp
description: BYOK model router for VS Code's Copilot chat, agent, and Ctrl+I — bring your own API keys or ChatGPT (Codex) / Claude.ai subscription. Bridge external tools like Claude Code onto the same providers. Plus Inquire inline edits.

wisp.provider.description: Active Provider — which Provider's base URL your API key is sent to. 'custom' uses wisp.baseUrl. Machine-scoped because selecting a Provider selects where your bearer key is sent, so a workspace must not be able to redirect it. Unknown values fall back to opencode-go.

wisp.baseUrl.description: OpenAI-compatible base URL used ONLY when the Active Provider is 'custom' (built-in Providers use their own hardcoded URLs and ignore this). Machine-scoped so a workspace cannot redirect requests (and the API key) to another endpoint.

wisp.model.description: Model id (bare, as served by /models — the endpoint rejects a provider-prefixed id). Run 'Wisp: List / Choose Model' to discover valid ids.

wisp.maxTokens.description: Max tokens per suggestion. 0 = no limit (lets multi-line and reasoning-model completions finish). A positive cap bounds latency but can truncate the answer mid-output.

wisp.temperature.description: Sampling temperature. Low keeps edits deterministic.

wisp.bridge.port.description: Port the Bridge listener binds on 127.0.0.1 (the local OpenAI-compatible endpoint external tools point at). Machine-scoped so a workspace cannot move the listener. Change it if the default clashes with another local service.

wisp.bridge.aliasPickerShowsModel.description: Show the alias's pinned model id in Claude Code's /model picker ('sol — gpt-5'). Off lists the bare alias name ('sol'). Claude Code fetches the list at startup, so a change shows after its next restart.

scripts.compile: tsc -p ./ && tsc -p webview && bun run bundle && vite build
